# **1\. admins Table**

To manage secure access to the admin dashboard.

| Column | Type | Description |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| id | UUID (PK) | Unique identifier |
| username | String | Unique username for login |
| password\_hash | String | Bcrypt hashed password |
| created\_at | Timestamp | Date of account creation |

# **2\. categories Table**

Manages the "Items required to purchase" (e.g., Brakes, Engine, Suspension). Supports sub-categories.

| Column | Type | Description |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| id | Int (PK) | Unique identifier |
| parent\_id | Int (FK) | Reference to categories.id (null for main categories) |
| name\_en | String | Category name in English (e.g., "Brake Pad Front") |
| name\_ar | String | Category name in Arabic (e.g., "فحمات فرامل أمامية") |
| slug | String | URL-friendly name (e.g., "brake-pads") |

# **3\. products Table**

The core inventory. Stores names, prices, and the Part Brand (e.g., Bosch) as a simple text field to save space.

| Column | Type | Description |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| id | UUID (PK) | Unique identifier |
| sku | String | Your internal stock number |
| oem\_number | String | Factory part number (Indexed for fast search) |
| category\_id | Int (FK) | Reference to categories.id |
| brand\_name | String | The manufacturer (e.g., "Bosch", "Brembo", "Genuine") |
| name\_en | String | Product name in English |
| name\_ar | String | Product name in Arabic |
| description\_en | Text | Detailed specs in English |
| description\_ar | Text | Detailed specs in Arabic |
| price | Decimal | Current selling price |
| stock\_quantity | Int | Available units in stock |
| is\_featured | Boolean | If true, shows on the homepage |
| is\_active | Boolean | If false, hidden from the shop  |
| dimensions | string | Dimensions of product |
| weight | float | Weight of product |
| manufactured\_in | string | Country of manufacture |
| condition | string |  Condition of product: “new” “used” |
| created\_at | date | Date of addition of product |
| updated\_at | date | Date of edit of product |

# **4\. product\_images Table**

Allows each product to have multiple photos for a gallery view.

| Column | Type | Description |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| id | UUID (PK) | Unique identifier |
| product\_id | UUID (FK) | Reference to products.id |
| url | String | The link to the image (S3 or local storage) |
| is\_main | Boolean | If true, used as the primary thumbnail |

# **5\. vehicles Table**

The "Car Library" table. This consolidated table powers the dropdown filters the client requested (Brand \> Series \> Specifics \> Chassis/Year).

| Column | Type | Description |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| id | Int (PK) | Unique identifier |
| brand | String | The Car Brand (e.g., "BMW", "Mercedes") |
| series | String | The Series (e.g., "5 Series", "7 Series") |
| specifics | String | The Engine/Trim (e.g., "520i", "530i", "740li") |
| chassis\_code | String | The Code (e.g., "F02", "G12", "W204") |
| year\_range | String | The Years (e.g., "2014-2020") |

# **6\. fitments Table**

The "Bridge" table. This connects a specific Part to a specific Car.

| Column | Type | Description |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| id | Int (PK) | Unique identifier |
| product\_id | UUID (FK) | Reference to products.id |
| vehicle\_id | Int (FK) | Reference to vehicles.id |

### **Why This Design Works**

**Bilingual Ready**  
 By storing name\_en and name\_ar in the same row, queries remain fast and simple. The application only needs to select the appropriate column based on the user’s language.

**Part Number (SKU) Indexing**  
 Customers often search using part numbers. Indexing the sku field ensures fast and efficient lookups.

### **Implementation Tip (Prisma Schema)**

Since the project uses TypeScript, Prisma provides a modern and type-safe way to manage PostgreSQL schemas.

model Product {  
 id             String         @id @default(uuid())  
 sku            String         @unique  
 brand          String?  
 nameEn         String  
 nameAr         String  
 descEn         String?  
 descAr         String?  
 price          Decimal  
 discountPrice  Decimal?  
 stockQuantity  Int            @default(0)  
 compatibility  String?  
 isFeatured     Boolean        @default(false)  
 categoryId     Int  
 category       Category       @relation(fields: \[categoryId\], references: \[id\])  
 images         ProductImage\[\]  
 createdAt      DateTime       @default(now())  
 updatedAt      DateTime       @updatedAt  
}

model Category {  
 id        Int       @id @default(autoincrement())  
 nameEn    String  
 nameAr    String  
 slug      String    @unique  
 products  Product\[\]  
}

model ProductImage {  
 id        String  @id @default(uuid())  
 url       String  
 isMain    Boolean @default(false)  
 productId String  
 product   Product @relation(fields: \[productId\], references: \[id\], onDelete: Cascade)  
}

**2\. Database Relationships**

## **1\. Category ↔ Product (One-to-Many)**

### **The Rule**

* One Category can have many Products  
* One Product belongs to exactly one Category

  ### **How It Works**

* The products table contains a category\_id field  
* This field references categories.id

  ### **In Action**

* When a user clicks on a category like "Brakes", the backend queries:  
* All products where category\_id \= Brakes category ID  
* This enables filtering products by category efficiently

  ## **2\. Category ↔ Category (Self-Referencing / Recursive)**

  ### **The Rule**

* A Category can have a parent category  
* A parent category can have multiple sub-categories

  ### **How It Works**

* The categories table includes a parent\_id field  
* This field references categories.id (within the same table)

  ### **In Action**

* Example:  
  * "Engine" → id: 10, parent\_id: null  
  * "Filters" → id: 20, parent\_id: 10  
* This structure defines "Filters" as a sub-category of "Engine"


  ## **3\. Product ↔ ProductImage (One-to-Many)**

  ### **The Rule**

* One Product can have multiple images (Gallery)  
* One Image belongs to exactly one Product

  ### **How It Works**

* The product\_images table contains a product\_id field  
* This field references products.id

  ### **In Action**

* When a user opens a product detail page (e.g., Bosch Spark Plug):  
* The system fetches all rows from product\_images where product\_id matches the selected product.  
* This allows the frontend to display a primary thumbnail and a supporting image gallery.


  ## **4\. Product ↔ Vehicle (Many-to-Many via Fitments)**

  ### **The Rule**

* One Product (e.g., an Oil Filter) can fit many different Cars.  
* One Car (e.g., BMW 520i) requires many different Products.

  ### **How It Works**

* This is handled by the "Bridge" table called fitments.  
* The fitments table contains two foreign keys: product\_id and vehicle\_id.

  ### **In Action**

* When a user selects "BMW" \> "5 Series" \> "520i" \> "G30" from the dropdowns:  
  1. The system finds the vehicle\_id for that specific car.  
  2. It looks into the fitments table for all product\_ids linked to that vehicle\_id.  
  3. It returns the list of parts that are 100% compatible with that specific car.


  ## **5\. Product ↔ Category (Filtering via Hierarchy)**

  ### **The Rule**

* When a user selects a Car, they should be able to narrow down results by "Item Type" (Category).

  ### **How It Works**

* This combines the fitments logic with the category\_id.

  ### **In Action**

* User wants "Brake Pads" for their "Mercedes W204":  
* The backend filters all products that are linked to the Mercedes W204 in the fitments table AND have the category\_id corresponding to "Brake Pads."  
*
